title: Linux Multi-Stage Attack Indicators (Combined Methods)
description: Detects hosts exhibiting multiple attack stage indicators: initial access (SSH failures), persistence (authorized_keys, crontab), execution (reverse shells, downloads). When 3+ different indicator types appear on the same host, it suggests an active multi-stage attack. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).
query: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Linux Syslog Logs' and (msg like '*Failed password*' or msg like '*authorized_keys*' or msg like '*/dev/tcp/*' or msg like '*curl*http*' or msg like '*wget*http*' or msg like '*crontab*' or msg like '*systemctl enable*' or msg like '*/etc/passwd*' or msg like '*/etc/shadow*') | stats count as total_indicators, distinctcount(msg) as unique_patterns by 'Host Name' | where unique_patterns > 3 | sort -total_indicators
